$comment: See ADR-0015 (tooling) and ADR-0019 (shape: consistency bounds). $id host is a placeholder identifier until the irrevon.dev domain purchase closes (ADR-0023). Fields client_ref_field and list_queryable implement amendment AM-9 (docs/review-queue.md, ratified 2026-07-21). Shape changes require an ADR.
title: Capability declaration
description: A destination's version-pinned, cited capability declaration (master doc §7.3, §7.5, §7.6): what tier it is, what idempotency it honors, what can be queried, whether the caller can stamp its own reference into the destination object, and what consistency bounds govern absence evidence. client_ref_field and list_queryable determine whether ORPHANED and LOST are even detectable; consistency bounds determine whether confirmed-absence can ever support automatic redispatch (RFC-002 §6).

destination.description: The destination API/product this declaration describes.

api_version.description: Pinned API version/namespace the declaration was verified against. Contract drift forces a declaration update, retest, and deviation ADR (§7.6).

tier.description: Capability tier (§7.5): C1 idempotency-keyed, C2 queryable, C3 opaque.

replay_semantics.description: What a replayed key returns (e.g. cached response incl. errors vs re-execution without side effects).

by.description: Keys the status query accepts (e.g. destination_ref, client_ref).

client_ref_field.description: Destination field where the caller can stamp its operation reference (strongest reconcile hook), or null if none exists (draft field, AM-9).

list_queryable.description: Whether destination effects can be LISTED in a window (distinct from status-by-id) — determines orphan-sweep feasibility (draft field, AM-9).

consistency.description: Cited or measured destination consistency bounds (ADR-0019, C1 finding M3). A null bound means no finite documented/measured value exists — confirmed-absence then supports human escalation only, never automatic redispatch (RFC-002 §6).

status_settlement_lag.description: Maximum documented/measured lag before a created effect is visible to the status query (ISO 8601 duration), or null when unbounded/unknown.

listing_lag.description: Maximum documented/measured lag before a created effect appears in list queries (ISO 8601 duration), or null when unbounded/unknown. Consumed by orphan-sweep windowing.

compensation_hook.description: Destination-native compensation operation, if any. Compensation is a new intent, never rollback (ADR-007).

citations.description: Primary-source URLs the declaration was verified against (§7.6).

evidence_quality.description: VF = explicit statement in destination docs; EI = evidence-backed inference (e.g. absence from a complete parameter list) — must be labeled per §7.6.
